title: model-guard: a cli-shaped tier denies Agent/Task, and the FIX line never names a phantom model
action: Two defects, one cell. (1) bee-model-guard.mjs:133 reads `stateLib.modelForTier(root, 'generation', 'claude') || "generation"`. When the generation tier is cli-shaped, modelForTier correctly returns null (state.mjs:703, by design — 'never a bogus model name'), so the deny message's FIX line tells the agent to `pass model: "generation"` — a model that does not exist. The guard's own remediation advice walks the agent into a second failure. (2) Per AO5, a dispatch declaring a tier must run on the model configured for that tier; when that tier resolves to {type:'cli'}, an Agent/Task dispatch is the WRONG TRANSPORT ENTIRELY and must be DENIED with a FIX pointing at the external-executor path (run the configured command via Bash, prompt on stdin, stdout is the digest — see the Delegation contract). Use resolveTier, not modelForTier, so the branch is TYPE-based, not string-based (plan.md test matrix, 'Config shapes'). Keep the hook fail-open on every unexpected condition: it must RETURN a typed deny, never throw (critical pattern 20260714 — a fail-open host swallows fail-closed throws). Mirror byte-for-byte into .bee/bin/hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs; they move together. Add test rows to hooks/test_model_guard.mjs: a cli-shaped generation tier + an anchored [bee-tier: generation] marker => DENIED, and the deny text contains no phantom model name; a cli-shaped tier + a model param => DENIED; a string-shaped tier => unchanged allow (regression). Include a sentinel-deny row so a crashed guard cannot read as green (critical pattern 20260714), and prove falsifiability once: break the new branch, watch the suite go red, restore byte-for-byte.

truths: A tier that resolves to {type:'cli'} denies an Agent/Task dispatch declaring it, and the deny explains the external-executor path
No deny message ever names a model that does not exist — the `|| "generation"` fallback is gone
The branch is type-based via resolveTier, never string-based
The guard still fail-opens on malformed input: typed deny, never a throw
prohibitions: Do NOT implement AO5's full model-equality rule (model param must equal the configured model) — that is Slice 2B, W1; it forces the unresolved 'is a marker now mandatory?' question
Do NOT let the guard auto-inject or rewrite a model param — deny only, per decision 0023
Do NOT weaken the anchored-marker forgery defence

verify: ! grep -qF '|| "generation"' hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs && grep -qF 'resolveTier' hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs && cmp -s hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs .bee/bin/hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs && node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs

trace.dropped_reason: Written against the Slice 2A plan that FAILED validation (reports/validation-slice-2a.md, 7 blockers). Re-plan from scratch; backlog row filed 2026-07-14. Not claimable as-is.
